rationale: Sui launched with a total fixed supply cap of 10 billion SUI; the foundation's tokenomics docs specify that staking rewards (the primary emission source) are drawn from a storage fund and a validator subsidy pool, with the subsidy component decreasing over time as the network matures. As of mid-2025, circulating supply is approximately 3.49–3.55 billion SUI against a total supply of ~10 billion, and annualised new issuance (staking rewards distributed on-chain) is estimated at roughly 6–7% of current circulating supply based on the disclosed ~5% target APY on ~70% of circulating supply being staked, implying net new tokens entering circulation at ~6.9% annualised; this rate declines as the circulating supply grows toward the 10 B cap and the subsidy schedule tapers.
caveats: Circulating supply growth (~27% YoY per CoinGecko history) also includes scheduled vesting unlocks from investor/team allocations, which are NOT new minting — they are pre-existing tokens entering circulation. Pure protocol minting (staking rewards only) is the ~7.5% figure above. If the question is total circulating supply growth rate, the figure would be higher (~20–27%) but is declining as large vesting tranches complete. The 0.075 figure reflects minted inflation only.
NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER_note: Precise real-time epoch-by-epoch minting data requires Sui RPC node queries or a paid analytics provider (e.g., Nansen, Token Terminal); the 7.5% is a best estimate from disclosed tokenomics parameters.
